tossups.question: This artist painted Diana over a fireplace among his frescoes for the bedroom of an abbess, the Camera di San Paolo. In a series preceded by his Venus and Cupid with a Satyr, this artist included a painting of a stormy cloud with a faintly visible humanoid face embracing a woman. This teacher of Parmigianino painted John the Evangelist so that he was only visible from a church’s choir in one fresco. Federico II Gonzaga commissioned this painter’s four-painting series that includes Danaë(“DAN-uh-ee”)and Jupiter and Io. In a major work by this artist, swirling layers of clouds disguise the octagonal architecture on which they are painted. For 10 points, name this Italian Mannerist painter of The Loves of Jupiter series, who used di sotto in su in his illusionistic ceiling frescoes, including his Assumption of the Virgin for Parma Cathedral.
tossups.question_sanitized: This artist painted Diana over a fireplace among his frescoes for the bedroom of an abbess, the Camera di San Paolo. In a series preceded by his Venus and Cupid with a Satyr, this artist included a painting of a stormy cloud with a faintly visible humanoid face embracing a woman. This teacher of Parmigianino painted John the Evangelist so that he was only visible from a church's choir in one fresco. Federico II Gonzaga commissioned this painter's four-painting series that includes Danae("DAN-uh-ee")and Jupiter and Io. In a major work by this artist, swirling layers of clouds disguise the octagonal architecture on which they are painted. For 10 points, name this Italian Mannerist painter of The Loves of Jupiter series, who used di sotto in su in his illusionistic ceiling frescoes, including his Assumption of the Virgin for Parma Cathedral.

packet.name: E. Prelims 5 - Florida, Georgia State, Illinois A, Toronto C

tossups.question: In one painting by this artist, a black robed man to the left has his arms crossed and is about to be whipped, while a figure in blue and orange to the right is being run through with a sword. He paired the four gospel writers with the four Doctors of the Church in the corners of a fresco for a dome that shows Christ appearing to fall from the center. This artist of Martyrdom of Four Saints and (*) Vision of St. John on Patmos depicted a spiralling series of figures that appear to jump out towards the viewer in his Assumption of the Virgin. He also showed a topless woman in a work in which an angel looks up towards a shower of gold and he painted a nude woman from behind being ravished by a nebulous cloud. For 10 points, name this painter of a series of works about the loves of Jupiter.
tossups.question_sanitized: In one painting by this artist, a black robed man to the left has his arms crossed and is about to be whipped, while a figure in blue and orange to the right is being run through with a sword. He paired the four gospel writers with the four Doctors of the Church in the corners of a fresco for a dome that shows Christ appearing to fall from the center. This artist of Martyrdom of Four Saints and (*) Vision of St. John on Patmos depicted a spiralling series of figures that appear to jump out towards the viewer in his Assumption of the Virgin. He also showed a topless woman in a work in which an angel looks up towards a shower of gold and he painted a nude woman from behind being ravished by a nebulous cloud. For 10 points, name this painter of a series of works about the loves of Jupiter.

bonuses.leadin: For the dome of the Parma Cathedral, this artist painted The Assumption of the Virgin as seen from below, or di sotto in sù(dee SOH-toh in soo). For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: For the dome of the Parma Cathedral, this artist painted The Assumption of the Virgin as seen from below, or di sotto in su(dee SOH-toh in soo). For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Italian Renaissance artist who included Ganymede (GAA-nih-meed) Abducted by the Eagle in his Ovid-inspired series Loves of Jupiter.

Jupiter and Juno appear above the three Fates on clouds in a painting titled for the "Destiny" of this person, part of a series which includes her being led off of a boat in The Disembarkation at Marseilles(mahr-SAY).
Jupiter holds a thunderbolt in a panel from Creation of the World by this artist who painted The School of Athens.

Jupiter holds a thunderbolt in a panel from Creation of the World by this artist who painted The School of Athens.
answers_sanitized: Marie de' Medici [prompt on partial answer]

bonuses.leadin: A prominent example of this technique is Andrea Mantegna’s depiction of many Cupids peeking down the oculus of the Camera degla Sposi. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this illusionistic technique in which the artist paints foreshortened figures to make it appear as if the figures in the painting are correctly proportioned when looking up at them. It literally means “from below, upward.”
This artist, born Antonio Allegri, made use of the di sotto in sù technique for his version of the Assumption of the Virgin in Parma Cathedral.

Di sotto in sú inspired this later illusionistic technique, in which the artist painted architectural features at the top of a ceiling to create an illusion. A prominent example of this technique is Pietro da Cortona’s frescoes for the Palazzo Barberini.

Di sotto in su inspired this later illusionistic technique, in which the artist painted architectural features at the top of a ceiling to create an illusion. A prominent example of this technique is Pietro da Cortona's frescoes for the Palazzo Barberini.

bonuses.leadin: Jupiter, in the form of an eagle, licks the arm of Ganymede as he carries him off in this artist's The Abduction of Ganymede. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this artist from Parma fond of dome painting. Another of his Jupiter paintings shows that God, in the form of a cloud, copulating with Io.

Correggio helped complete the family chapel of this artist in Mantua. This artist depicted putto sitting on the painted ceiling of his marvelous Camera degli Sposi and showed a supine Jesus in Dead Christ.

This later painter from Parma was heavily influenced by Correggio's dome paintings for his own Baroque dome paintings, like the one he executed for the San Andrea della Valle in Rome. This rival of Domenichino and member of the Bolognese school made The Gods of Olympus for the Villa Borghese.
answers: Giovanni Lanfranco [or Giovanni di Steffano or Il Cavaliere Giovanni Lanfranchi]
answers_sanitized: Giovanni Lanfranco [or Giovanni di Steffano or Il Cavaliere Giovanni Lanfranchi]

bonuses.leadin: Humor quizbowl’s number one Scipione Borghese fan by naming the following artists of paintings now located in the Galleria Borghese, for 10 points each.
parts: Aphrodite and Cupid sit beside Laura Bagarotto on an open sarcophagus in this artist’s Sacred and Profane Love, which is now housed at the Galleria Borghese. This Venetian artist also painted The Rape of Europa.
The Gallery also houses this artist’s Venus and Cupid With a Honeycomb. Judith calmly rests her arm on Holofernes’ head in the depiction of that scene by this court painter to Frederick the Wise.

This artist’s Danae, in which Cupid and Danae receive Jupiter in the form of a golden shower, is part of the Borghese collection. This man painted the Assumption of the Virgin for the dome of the Parma cathedral.
answers: Lucas Cranach the Elder [accept no other Cranachs]
answers_sanitized: Titian [accept Tiziano Vecelli or Teziano Vecellio]
Lucas Cranach the Elder [accept no other Cranachs]

bonuses.leadin: Name these artists who created works entitled Assumption of the Virgin, for 10 points each.
parts: This artist's Assumption of the Virgin was created for a church in Toledo, where he lived. This artist born on Crete also painted The Disrobing of Christ and The Burial of Count Orgaz.
One work by this artist inspired Manet's Olympia, and this artist's Assumption of the Virgin is the largest altarpiece in Venice, which is also the location of his Pesaro Altarpiece.
This artist's Assumption of the Virgin decorates the dome of a Parma cathedral. This artist depicted Io, Leda, Danae, and Ganymede in four paintings known as the Loves of Jupiter series.
answers_sanitized: Titian [or Tiziano Vecelli; or Tiziano Vecellio]
